description_long: This object is a small bird with a streamlined body, olive-green upperparts, and a lighter, almost yellowish underbelly. The head is rounded with a short, conical beak and a subtle pale patch around the eye. The wings are compact and closely held to the body, blending into the overall coloration with some faint brownish and yellowish tones. The tail is straight and moderately long, tapering to a rounded tip, and the legs are short and not prominently visible. The plumage appears smooth and uniform, with no distinct markings or patterns, giving the bird a sleek and subtle appearance. The overall form and coloration suggest a species adapted to blending into leafy urban or park environments.
description: A small, olive-green bird with a pale underbelly, short conical beak, and smooth, uniform plumage. Its streamlined body and rounded tail give it a subtle, natural look.
description_view_0: Side view showing the bird's streamlined body, olive-green back, and pale underbelly with the beak and eye clearly visible.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the rounded tail and the symmetrical alignment of the wings and body.
description_view_2: Opposite side view similar to view 0, showing the other profile of the bird with consistent coloration and body shape.
description_view_3: Front view focusing on the beak, rounded head, and the subtle transition of color from the head to the body.
attribute_plumage_pattern: solid olive-green upperparts with a pale, gradient underbelly and faint yellowish tones
